gaps.theme: Voice & humor mechanics
gaps.tactic: Wit emerges from precision / logic; humor never the goal
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Humor must emerge from precision and rigorous argument, never be the objective. Levine footnote-jokes work because the main argument is rigorous. The deadpan structure: setup = a logical premise stated plainly, 'joke' = the unavoidable conclusion landed flatly with no editorializing/wink ('a circular argument, delivered politely'). Budget ~0-1 per piece, often zero; at most 1 dry aside per unit and only when the substance earns it.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md (logical reductio, wit-from-precision); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §0 (funny-by-framing, max 1 aside); comparison-snippet-voice.md (humor from data, max 1 joke, one word doing the work); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-15.md §7.3 (the joke is one word inside a sentence, no setup/punchline)
gaps.why: design.md §10 already has 'Humor from logic, rarely' with a 0-1 budget and 'no wink' — this is COVERED at the budget level. What is UNDER-SPECIFIED is the mechanic: the deadpan structure (premise stated, conclusion landed flatly), 'one word doing the work, no setup/punchline,' and the rule that humor must arise from rigor not commentary. Worth a one-line addition to §10's humor bullet.

gaps.theme: Voice register — anti-marketing / committee / pitch-deck
gaps.tactic: Avoid committee-bland AND sales-pitch registers; brochure test
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Two named failure registers to avoid: the 'committee-written Morningstar feel' (uniform, third-person, characterless) and the Motley-Fool pitch shape. Concrete brochure test: 'If a copy line could appear in a brochure for a generic SaaS product, rewrite it.' No pitch-deck voice, no generic-SaaS-upsell lines.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md (committee/Motley-Fool); UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE.md ('brochure for a generic SaaS product' test); BRAND.md §5 (retire generic SaaS upsell voice); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (sober evidence-first alternative)
gaps.why: design.md §6 bans hype/superlatives/FOMO and §2 sets a calm/dry voice, but it gives no explicit anti-register test. The brochure test is a concrete, portable self-check that the current file lacks.

gaps.theme: Word choice — full Orwell six-rule set
gaps.tactic: Orwell rules #1, #5, #6 (the three not yet in design.md)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Orwell's six rules: #1 never use a metaphor you're used to seeing in print (COIN new images, don't reuse), #2 short word, #3 cut every cuttable word, #4 active voice, #5 never a foreign phrase / scientific / jargon word when an everyday English equivalent exists, #6 break any rule sooner than say something outright barbarous (the pragmatism override — rules are guidelines not absolutes).
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §4 (all six rules); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 Pass C (Orwell six); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL / TONE-OVERHAUL (rules #2,#4 shipped)
gaps.why: design.md §4 covers #2 (short word), #3 (cut filler — partially, via kill-list), #4 (active voice). MISSING outright: #5 (foreign/scientific/jargon → everyday equivalent, distinct from the Latinate-precision carve-out), and #6 (the pragmatism override — explicitly NO 'break a rule rather than be barbarous' escape hatch exists in design.md, which currently reads as absolute). #1 (fresh-metaphor permission) is partly in §5 but the 'coin new images' positive instruction is absent.

gaps.theme: Word choice — cut-every-cuttable-word as a per-sentence pass
gaps.tactic: Orwell #3 as an active per-sentence prune, not just a kill-list
gaps.boothcheck_detail: After writing each sentence, ask whether any word can be cut without losing meaning; if it can, cut it. Graham: 'the best writing is what remains after you've cut everything that can be cut.' This is the BROAD principle; the banned-phrase kill-list is only its narrow instance.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §4/§9 (per-sentence pruning); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 Pass C; V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-RESEARCH.md ('Don't pad')
gaps.why: design.md §4 has a fixed 'filler kill-list (delete on sight)' but never states the general per-sentence prune that generates such lists. The procedural habit (interrogate each sentence for a cuttable word) is the transferable rule and is absent.

gaps.theme: Word choice — drop spoken fillers, conversational-not-literal-speech
gaps.tactic: Write conversationally but tighter than speech; drop um/sort-of/you-know
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Aim for conversational tone but MORE structured than actual speech (Graham: 'write like you speak, but tighten it further'). Drop spoken fillers: 'um,' 'sort of,' 'you know.' Boyle's conversational fillers and self-correcting asides do NOT transfer to written work. Also no manufactured casual bravado ('Let's get that out the way.') in an otherwise formal piece — a tonal inconsistency.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §1/§5 (conversational-not-literal filter); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST.md (Report B casual bravado as a tell)
gaps.why: No trace in design.md. The read-aloud rule (§11) is adjacent but the specific 'tighter than speech, drop spoken fillers, no casual-bravado aside' guidance is absent.

gaps.theme: Audience model
gaps.tactic: Write to a smart non-specialist (Buffett's two-sisters model)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Maintain an accessible voice without dumbing down: imagine writing to a smart reader who doesn't know the terminology and needs things explained without condescension. The fix for an over-complex explanation is to 'simplify by trusting the reader more, not by adding hedging or apology.' No condescension; trust the reader. Tooltips/explanations only where the WHAT genuinely needs explaining, never the obvious.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §3 (Buffett two sisters); UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE.md (no condescension; trust the reader; explain only non-obvious); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.4 (layer/progressively disclose, avoid TMI)
gaps.why: design.md never names an audience model or the 'simplify by trusting, not hedging' rule. §8 (honesty about limits) and §4 (insincerity test) are adjacent but the explicit no-condescension / accessible-without-dumbing-down stance is absent.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — preserve ethos / analytical-authority posture
gaps.tactic: Build ethos via specificity, intellectual honesty, technical accuracy, measured tone, calibration
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Keep the analytical-authority (ethos) posture; do not swing to storytelling ('let me tell you a story about Tesla'). Ethos is built through five sources: specificity (exact, correctly-cited numbers), intellectual honesty (acknowledging limits/counterarguments), technical accuracy (no errors, terms used correctly), measured tone (no hype/alarmism), authoritative calibration (not overselling). The analytical posture IS the product differentiation vs hype.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §6 (Aristotle ethos); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (deadpan analytical, no consumer-app chrome)
gaps.why: design.md §10 has specialist-defensibility and §8 honesty-about-limits, which cover two of the five ethos sources. The unifying ethos frame and the explicit 'don't swing to storytelling / keep the analytical posture' instruction are not stated.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — logos is native strength, don't dilute
gaps.tactic: Lead with the logical case; don't trade rigor for style
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Logos (the analytical case) is the native strength — built through concrete numbers + their implications, cause-and-effect reasoning, counterargument engagement, framework consistency. Earn conclusions from evidence rather than asserting them (logos over pathos). Prose must not trade logical rigor for style.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §6 (Aristotle logos); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST.md (Report C earns conclusions vs Report B sentiment-led)
gaps.why: design.md §2 (descriptive not rhetorical, let the reader draw the conclusion) + §10 (pathos via consequence, the most-missed) + §9 (translate before you cite) together cover this. Listed so the verifier confirms no over-claim is needed.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — counter-argument engagement WITHIN a one-sided section
gaps.tactic: Acknowledge the opposing case inside your own case (refutational two-sidedness), but watch double-counting
gaps.boothcheck_detail: In an argumentative section, acknowledge a specific piece of the opposing-case evidence and explain why your view still holds — strengthens ethos and logos. Refutational two-sidedness (raise the negative, then address it) beats mere confession; disclosing a real weakness raises credibility for skeptical readers, especially when the source is presumed biased. NOTE the failure mode boothcheck flagged: double-counting the same evidence in both bull and bear creates contradiction-within-document — handle carefully.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §7/§11 (WSJ alternation, double-accounting risk); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §0.2/§1.5 (two-sided/refutational, STRONG, Eisend meta-analysis; Crowley & Hoyer); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (bear-case spine concedes then refutes)
gaps.why: design.md §10 has 'anticipate and answer the strongest objection' — close but framed as objection-handling generally. MISSING: the stronger refutational-two-sidedness finding (admit a real weakness to raise credibility), the 'presumed biased' moderator, and the double-counting caution.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — caveat placement
gaps.tactic: Place caveats last, voluntary, lower-importance, refutational, bounded-numeric
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Every caveat goes LAST, reads as VOLUNTARY, is framed as LOWER-importance, and is refutational (name the limit, then the guardrail). A voluntarily-disclosed real limit is the integrity belief made audible. Disclose REAL limits; never engineer a token/fake flaw (the blemishing bump does not fire for high-effort skeptics).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.5 (placement rule, last/voluntary/refutational/bounded-numeric; Ein-Gar token-flaw caveat); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (candor-about-breakage IS the voice); BRAND.md §5
gaps.why: design.md §8 says 'say the limit out loud' but gives no placement/framing procedure and no 'don't engineer a fake flaw' guard. This is a concrete, transferable addition to §8.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — frame a refusal/limit as a superior method
gaps.tactic: Disclose-as-strength; frame a withheld output as deliberate, never as a shortcoming
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When withholding something (a verdict, a number, a credential), frame it as a deliberately superior method with a clear payoff ('we show the question the price is asking, not a fake answer'), never as a confession or apology. Voice must be non-self-deprecating: 'an independent research project,' NOT 'just a hobbyist.' A removed conflict beats a disclosed one.
gaps.boothcheck_source: LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT §1 (disclose-as-strength, positive valence, Cain-Loewenstein-Moore); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.5/§1.7 (frame refusal as superior method; lead with structural independence); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §1 (understatement w/ precision)
gaps.why: Distinct from §8 'honesty about limits' — this is the FRAMING of a limit/refusal as a positive-valence deliberate strength rather than an apology. design.md has no positive-valence framing rule. Highly portable to any product that withholds an output by design.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — anti-false-uncertainty / no over-hedging
gaps.tactic: Don't hedge every sentence; reserve hedges for genuine unknowns
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Over-hedging ('may,' 'could,' 'potentially' everywhere) creates FALSE uncertainty and is its own failure. State what you know confidently, state what you don't know explicitly, never conflate the two. 'We don't know yet' is stronger than 'may potentially eventually develop.' No stacked qualifiers ('could, may, might, perhaps') that say nothing — state it or cut it.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §8 (anti-false-uncertainty); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 Pass D (no hedging stacks); EDITING-PROMPT.md (no added boilerplate hedges); TONE-OVERHAUL (acknowledged uncertainty over reflexive hedging)
gaps.why: design.md §8 ('more credible than hedging every sentence with may/could/potentially') + §4 insincerity test + §5 'no filler hedges' cover this well. Listed to confirm we are not over-claiming a gap.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — empirical observation over speculation
gaps.tactic: Let evidence land without closing the case prematurely
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Favor empirical observation over speculation; end on 'we'll see how this develops' rather than a forced verdict when the evidence doesn't support one. Don't manufacture a conclusion the data can't bear.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §1 (Boyle empirical-over-speculation); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.2 (descriptive mode: let the reader draw conclusions)
gaps.why: design.md §2 (descriptive, let the reader draw the conclusion) + §8 (say the limit) + §9 descriptive section mode cover this. Confirming, not flagging.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — lede+nut-graf two-beat detail
gaps.tactic: Sentence 1 = lede (concrete hook, NOT thesis); sentences 2-3 = nut graf (thesis + stakes + why-now); para 2+ = evidence
gaps.boothcheck_detail: The narrative opening has a precise three-part shape: S1 is a specific concrete hook that invites the reader in and is explicitly NOT the verdict; S2-3 make the thesis explicit and say why it matters and why now; para 2+ is the evidence body for that thesis. Opening with specificity beats opening with summary.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §7 (worked NEE example); TONE-OVERHAUL (item 9); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.6 (story-shaped openings)
gaps.why: design.md §9 already specifies 'lede+nut-graf for narrative: sentence one is a concrete hook (not yet the thesis), sentences two-three make the thesis and stakes explicit, then the body.' Fully covered.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — open on the analytical question, not a number wall
gaps.tactic: Lead with the question/implication; numbers arrive after framing
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Valuation/data prose should open by NAMING the analytical question (or the implication/what the situation means) rather than dumping figures. 'Open on the analytical question, not a number wall.' Frame the spread/tension as the analysis itself rather than listing data. Close on the load-bearing assumption / where the model is most fragile.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION-PROGRESS.md (open on analytical question, close on load-bearing assumption); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.4 (turtle-boil, no ratio wall); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §2
gaps.why: design.md §2 (Feynman rhythm, never open on a wall of numbers, never stack three stats as first clause) + §9 (translate before you cite, numbers in supporting clauses) cover the no-number-wall opening. Confirming.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — ratio-wall / turtle-boil named pattern
gaps.tactic: Ban the 'ratio wall'; introduce numbers one beat at a time with framing for each
gaps.boothcheck_detail: A 'ratio wall' = 5+ ratios in 2-3 sentences (the audited examples packed 4-6 ratios in 2-3 sentences) and is an explicit AI tell a reader skips. 'Turtle-boil' the numbers: lead with the implication, then bring evidence one beat at a time, with brief framing for each; do not lead with the headline ratio.
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-15.md §1.8/§3.3/§7.4 (ratio-wall, 5+ ratios, turtle-boil); TONE-OVERHAUL (WMT anchor turtle-boil); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §2
gaps.why: design.md §2 bans 'a wall of numbers' and 'three stats as the first clause' but gives no quantified threshold. The concrete 5+-ratios-in-2-3-sentences definition and the 'brief framing for each number' instruction sharpen §2.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — anti-curiosity-gap, with the safe-form carve-out
gaps.tactic: No teaser/clickbait; curiosity only as an immediately-closed micro-gap
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Reject curiosity-gap/teaser section titles and ledes ('the surprising reason this is mispriced') — they trip the clickbait failure and corrode analytical authority. Use curiosity only in its SAFE form: a GENUINE question the very next sentences actually answer (immediately-closed micro-gap), never a withholding tease. 'Decks'/pull-forward lines must preview a real answer, never repeat or tease. Open loops/cliffhangers are rejected (Zeigarnik recall is debunked; what replicates is resumption — serve it via honest re-entry points).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §1.9/§2.9/§4A (Upworthy 8,977 A/B: vagueness helped 8.7%, hurt 50.9%; Zeigarnik null; Ovsiankina resumption); design.md §9 (anti-tease, partial)
gaps.why: design.md §9 already bans teasing ('the surprising reason this is mispriced... that's a curiosity gap, not BLUF') — COVERED at the ban level. UNDER-SPECIFIED: the SAFE-FORM carve-out (a genuine immediately-answered micro-gap is allowed) and the explicit no-cliffhanger/open-loop rule are not stated. Worth one clause in §9.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — structure-first gets read MORE (the empirical backing)
gaps.tactic: BLUF does not lose readers; front-loaded structure is read more
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Leading with the conclusion does NOT make readers leave; structure-first content gets read more, and a casual reader leaving satisfied is a win. The top is the scarcest attention (~57% of viewing above the fold, ~74% in first two screenfuls — treat as a gradient artifact, not a law). Front-load the answer AND advertise the depth below (scent) so the reader chooses to descend.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §1.4/§1.5 (NN/g scrolling+attention; inverted pyramid; gradient-not-law)
gaps.why: design.md §9 already asserts 'leading with the conclusion does not make readers leave — structure-first content gets read more.' The fold-percentage/scent material is reading-LAYOUT (presentation), not a prose rule, so it does not belong in design.md.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — scale anchor on every load-bearing number
gaps.tactic: Pair every figure with a reference point that gives it scale/meaning
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Rarely state a number without saying what it means; the comparison is the teaching. '$2B in operating earnings' → '$2B, roughly all the profits insurance generated in the 1990s.' Give every load-bearing number a scale anchor.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §3 (Buffett numbers-with-context); design.md §9
gaps.why: design.md §9 already requires 'give every load-bearing number a scale anchor ("$2B, about what the whole division earned last decade").' Fully covered.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — percentage-points vs percent
gaps.tactic: Difference of two percentages is percentage points (pp), never %
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Levels are %; the DIFFERENCE between two percentages is percentage POINTS, written 'pp' (e.g. +2.7pp), never '%'. Rendering a delta with '%' reads as 'X percent more' instead of 'X points more' and is wrong-unit. Also: 'percentage points'/'pt' for rate gaps; '%/yr' for annualized rates; label the period.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL.md §2.3 (hard rule, +2.7pp, fmtSignedPp); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN.md; V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §5.3; design.md §12
gaps.why: design.md §12 already has 'the difference between two percentages is percentage points ("+2.7pp"), not "%".' Covered; '%/yr for annualized' is a minor sub-detail not worth a separate gap.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — distinguish similar-but-distinct metrics
gaps.tactic: Don't conflate metrics with similar names; don't substitute one for another
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Distinct metrics with similar names must not be substituted: GAAP vs non-GAAP; EV/EBITDA vs EV/Operating-Income; net margin vs operating margin; summed-quarterly-EPS P/E drifts low on diluting companies; deposits are not 'debt'; a salience measure (share of comments mentioning X) is not an effect size or a population share. Always say which metric and on what basis.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 1 (EV/EBITDA vs EV/OI; P/E drift); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (GAAP vs non-GAAP marked); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (banks: 'net margin' banned); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.8 (Fogg 46.1% is salience not magnitude); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (base-rate bucket honesty)
gaps.why: design.md §12 covers period/unit labeling and cross-document consistency but NOT the metric-conflation hazard (using a similar-named or wrong-basis metric, or restating a salience % as a magnitude). This is a general numeric-honesty rule, finance examples aside.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — bounded numeric uncertainty over verbal hedging or false precision
gaps.tactic: Report a range/distribution, not a false-precise point; never a fake single number
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Present uncertainty as a BOUNDED NUMERIC range/band (best-and-worst), never as a false-precise point value and never as verbal hand-wringing ('no one can really know'). Numeric-range trust cost is small (d≈-0.12 to -0.15); verbal hedging is large (d≈-0.55). A single point value where the honest answer is a range is false precision. Surface dispersion (percentiles, HDI) around a central estimate.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.5 (bounded numeric, Howe 2019, van der Bles 2020 N=5,780); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (Report C distribution/HDI); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §1.8 (lead with disagreement); REPORT-POLISH-TODO §5 (make the disagreement the centerpiece)
gaps.why: design.md §12 handles implausible figures and consistency but has NO rule that uncertainty should be a bounded numeric range rather than a false-precise point or verbal hedge. This is a strong, generalizable numeric-honesty rule with a measured effect size, absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — show the adjustment, justify any substituted input
gaps.tactic: When a figure is normalized/adjusted/substituted, disclose both the raw and adjusted with a one-clause reason
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Never silently substitute or clean a number. When you swap reported for adjusted ($10.9B used vs $10.3B reported because OCF-minus-depreciation is a better steady-state proxy), state the swap AND the reason. When you normalize (mid-cycle vs trough margin), show BOTH the adjusted comparator and the raw figure plus a one-clause reason; showing only the cleaned number is its own dishonesty and can manufacture conviction. Never show a misleading bare figure (a charge-depressed -1.1% margin) without its reason.
gaps.boothcheck_source: BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (justify adjusted input, $10.9B vs $10.3B); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §4.4 (always show the adjustment; never bare misleading figure); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN.md (trailing-vs-normalized must be labeled); REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (show only what's real)
gaps.why: design.md §12 has nothing on disclosing adjustments/normalizations. The 'show both raw and adjusted with a reason; never a bare misleading figure' rule is a distinct, portable numeric-honesty discipline absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — recompute dependent figures after a base correction
gaps.tactic: Correcting a base figure obligates re-deriving everything derived from it
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Percentage derivatives that no longer match after an earlier number was corrected must be re-derived (e.g. '% below current price' computed against a now-changed fair value). A base correction propagates to every dependent figure.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 1 (recompute dependent percentages); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (re-bake propagates)
gaps.why: design.md §12 requires cross-document consistency but not the active obligation to recompute derived figures after correcting a base. A subtle but real numeric-honesty gap.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — cite the authoritative figure, not a self-recomputed one
gaps.tactic: When an authoritative source figure exists, use it rather than recomputing a near-equivalent
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Do not compute your own weighted-average / near-equivalent of visible outputs and cite that; cite the authoritative value (the data-block-header / blended.base). Tie-breaker for cross-section disagreement: the figure matching the authoritative source is canonical, align the others to it.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 1/Rule 4 (cite blended.base; data-block-header tie-breaker); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (prefer deterministic header value as canonical)
gaps.why: design.md §12 says the same figure must agree everywhere but gives no tie-breaker rule (which value is authoritative when they disagree) and no 'don't recompute a near-equivalent' rule. Useful, portable addition.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — report n / denominator with every rate
gaps.tactic: Show the sample size and denominator behind every percentage; down-weight small-n
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Never present a rate/percentage without its denominator and (for base rates) its sample size and time window. Show numerator and denominator together (144% = $10.3B FCF / $7.2B NI). Tag low-n figures as low-confidence ('overfit territory'); base rates carry their bucket denominator and window ('N% at this level sustained it a decade'). Decompose a headline ratio into its drivers (DuPont) so it can be audited.
gaps.boothcheck_source: BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (denominator visible; ROE DuPont decompose); REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (report n with every cell, n>=30 robust); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL §6 (base-rate denominator + window); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.8 (report ranks #2-#9 alongside the headline)
gaps.why: design.md §9 covers scale anchors and §12 covers period/unit labels, but neither requires surfacing the DENOMINATOR / sample size behind a derived percentage, nor decomposing a headline ratio. Distinct numeric-honesty discipline.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — null handling in a claim sentence
gaps.tactic: When a value is null, drop the element; never render an em-dash/dash gap inside a claim
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Assemble a claim sentence only from non-null fields; if a field is null, drop its clause/pill rather than render a '—' or em-dash gap, which reads as broken. Don't emit a number-shaped field (a null margin-of-safety) into a feed — remove it. Don't fabricate a default-zero ('+0.0%') that presents absent data as a real value.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §2.5 (null-handling, no fabricated precision); BRAND-CAMPAIGN (removed null MoS from feeds); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL §6 ('?? 0' default-zero flagged); design.md §12
gaps.why: design.md §12 already says 'when a value is null, drop it cleanly — don't render an em-dash gap inside a claim sentence, which reads as broken.' The default-zero sub-case is a minor extension not worth a separate gap.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — spell out cryptic quantities / decode shorthand
gaps.tactic: Replace terse shorthand with plain language saying WHAT holds and FOR HOW LONG
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Replace cryptic shorthand ('11.3y must persist') with plain phrasing ('the price assumes this margin holds about 11 years before fading'). Cap an implausible/non-physical computed figure into words ('beyond any margin a comparable business earns') rather than printing fake precision (a '351.8% operating margin').
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §2.4 (decode '11.3y'); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN.md (duration clarity); design.md §12 (spell out cryptic quantities)
gaps.why: design.md §12 already has 'spell out cryptic quantities ("hold a 9.7% margin for ~11 years," not "11.3y must persist").' The fabricated-precision-into-words sub-rule overlaps with §12's implausible-figure rule. Covered.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — every number traces to a source / no recalled figures
gaps.tactic: Every number traces to provided data, a cited source, or a defensible lookup; never a recalled figure
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Every number must trace to a real source; if you can't source it, don't write it. Never cite a training-data/recalled/from-memory number; hedging it with 'roughly' does not launder it. A wrong number in the first sentence loses the reader for good. Allowed source classes are explicitly bounded (e.g. the data/EDGAR block, a cache, or a defensible looked-up figure).
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 1; NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §0/Pass A; V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (SMUGGLING TEST); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (every number traces to a linked EDGAR filing); design.md §12
gaps.why: design.md §12 already: 'Every number is sourced... Never state a recalled training-data number as fact, and hedging it with "roughly" does not launder it.' Fully covered.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — name the missing data instead of inventing it
gaps.tactic: When a figure isn't available, say so and confine the claim to what the data supports
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When a figure/peer-comparison/base isn't in the authoritative data, state that plainly and limit the claim to what is supported ('the directional concern stands regardless'). Flag undisclosed denominators ('doubling from an undisclosed base... limits what the metric reveals'). A fabricated quote or number is worse than none — under any outage, write without the figure rather than inventing one.
gaps.boothcheck_source: BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (Report C names missing data; flags undisclosed base); REPORT-FILING-EVIDENCE.md §5 (never fabricate; graceful degrade); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §2.2 (say plainly why models can't price it)
gaps.why: design.md §12 covers not-inventing numbers but NOT the positive instruction to NAME the gap and bound the claim (the 'absence of evidence is reported, not filled' move), nor the 'fabricated > none' framing, nor flagging an undisclosed denominator. A distinct anti-smuggling rule.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — mark a real-but-weakly-informative figure as not-decision-relevant
gaps.tactic: When a number is real but weakly informative, neutralize over-reading it in the same breath
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When a figure is accurate but says little, state it then immediately note its limited relevance ('though this is not particularly relevant given the dynamics of the customer base'; 'from an undisclosed base... limits how much the metric reveals') so it is not smuggled in as strong evidence.
gaps.boothcheck_source: BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (mark figure not-relevant; Report A retention + Report C doubling)
gaps.why: No trace in design.md. Distinct from naming missing data — this is about a present figure being quietly over-weighted. A subtle, portable honesty rule.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — no header/badge duplication in prose
gaps.tactic: Don't restate values already shown structurally (badges, headers); explain them, don't repeat
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Reader-facing prose must not restate values already presented as UI badges/headers (verdict, score, fair value, current price, tier). Restating them reads as filler; the prose's job is to contextualize and explain those values, not contest or repeat them.
gaps.boothcheck_source: TONE-OVERHAUL (header-duplication rule); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (don't restate the deterministic header — reads as filler)
gaps.why: design.md has no rule against prose duplicating structurally-displayed values. Generalizes to any product where prose sits next to a data display (in-product strings, dashboards). Worth a line, though somewhat product-shaped.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — asymmetric argument / why-discipline
gaps.tactic: Name the mechanism/cause; never fall back to a vague 'direction is uneven'
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When explaining why something happened, cite the cause where it is attributable and name the mechanism rather than gesturing at it. Banned fallback: 'direction is uneven' (in place of a named cause). No generic risk-factor language that says nothing specific ('exposed to any deceleration in consumer spending'). No pseudo-texture specifics that don't earn their place unless they form a pattern.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md (asymmetric argument + why-discipline); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §3.2 (no generic risk language; no pseudo-Boyle texture)
gaps.why: design.md §10 'name the incentive (who benefits, who pays)' is adjacent but narrower. The broader rule — name the MECHANISM/cause, ban vague causal fallbacks and generic boilerplate risk language — is not stated. Worth folding into §10.

gaps.theme: Editing process — write bad fast, then iterate
gaps.tactic: First drafts are vessels; the editing is where the writing happens
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Write a bad version quickly, then iterate through multiple passes of elimination. First drafts carry ideas, not the final prose; the writing happens in the edit. (Pairs with the existing ordered-edit-passes and edit-don't-regenerate rules.)
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §5 (Graham: edit is where writing happens); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (test-then-iterate)
gaps.why: design.md §11 covers ordered passes and edit-don't-regenerate but never states the draft-fast-then-edit workflow. Minor, but a distinct process principle.

gaps.theme: Editing process — ordered edit passes A-E
gaps.tactic: Accuracy first, polish last: (A) accuracy/no-smuggling (B) em-dash/punctuation/lint (C) concision (D) voice/variation (E) read-aloud/scannability
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Run the edit as distinct passes in a fixed order, edit ruthlessly each pass: A accuracy/no smuggled facts → B em-dash + punctuation + lint → C cut padding/tighten → D voice + variation → E read-aloud + scannability. Don't polish prose you haven't fact-checked. EDITING-PROMPT's variant: read whole → numeric → methodology-leak → em-dash → cross-section consistency → voice.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 (5 passes A-E); EDITING-PROMPT.md (ordered workflow); design.md §11
gaps.why: design.md §11 already has the exact A-E ordered passes. Fully covered.

gaps.theme: Editing process — read the whole thing, don't skim
gaps.tactic: First step of any review: read the full text, don't skim
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Before editing, read the full text the way a reader would, not a skim. The final-check is a fixed confirm-before-emit list (a clean pass returning zero edits is a valid result).
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md (workflow step 1, 'Don't skim'; empty-edits valid); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (read each in isolation)
gaps.why: design.md §11 lists passes but never states 'read it whole first, don't skim' or 'zero edits is a valid outcome.' Small process addition.

gaps.theme: Editing process — edit, don't regenerate (surgical substitutions)
gaps.tactic: Fix a flawed draft with exact sentence-level find/replace, not a full rewrite
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Fix specific failure classes (numeric drift, tone slips, methodology leaks, em-dash prose, contradictions) with surgical sentence-level substitutions, preserving the parts that worked and keeping each change reviewable. Edit-vs-regenerate also frames cheap in-place reconciliation as the default for catchable surface defects, escalating to a rewrite only if needed.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md (edit don't regenerate); design.md §11; V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (prefer in-place reconciliation); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §11
gaps.why: design.md §11 already: 'Edit, don't regenerate. Fix a flawed draft with surgical sentence-level substitutions, not a full rewrite.' Covered.

gaps.theme: Editing process — pair every ban with a positive-rewrite example
gaps.tactic: A bare prohibition makes the writer invent a new evasion; show the preferred phrasing
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Each banned phrasing should be paired with a positive-rewrite example so there is somewhere to anchor instead of generating a novel evasion. Applied to em-dash, recalled-history, data-absence, terminal-multiple rules. (This is a meta-rule about how to WRITE the standard itself, and a self-correction trigger: if you catch yourself drifting into a banned register in a replacement, rewrite it in the voice of the sentence it replaces.)
gaps.boothcheck_source: V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (ban + positive-rewrite anchor); EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 5 (self-correction trigger on register drift)
gaps.why: design.md §1 already pairs the em-dash ban with rewrite examples (good practice in evidence) but never states the META-rule (always pair a ban with a rewrite). The self-correction-on-drift trigger is also absent. A worthwhile authoring principle for the standard.

gaps.theme: Editing process — gate mechanical rules with a script (prose-gate)
gaps.tactic: Run the em-dash + repeated-shape + banned-template + smuggling-vocab sweep as one command before shipping
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Gate the mechanical rules automatically: `node scripts/prose-gate.mjs <file>` bundles the em-dash sweep, the repeated opening/closing-shape check (lint-prose-variation.mjs, keyed off the canonical angle deck), the banned-template-phrase sweep, and the smuggling/judgment-vocabulary sweep. Exit 0 = clean; fail-closed (any check flags → block); fix and re-run until clean. Tunable --open-max/--close-max caps. Banned-list kept short + high-confidence (a false positive blocks a ship). Smuggling sweep runs over extracted prose records only (audience-aware: same words OK internally, banned user-facing).
gaps.boothcheck_source: prose-gate.mjs (full spec); comparison-snippet-voice.md (gate before shipping); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 Pass B; design.md §11
gaps.why: design.md §11 already names the script gate and the three checks (em-dash, repeated opening/closing shapes, banned-template). The smuggling-vocab sweep and fail-closed/short-list mechanics are sub-details; the principle is covered.

gaps.theme: Editing process — concrete banned-template phrase regexes
gaps.tactic: Hardened literal template phrases a grammatical-shape check misses
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Beyond grammatical-shape repetition, ban specific hardened literal phrasings: 'one is X, the other Y' closer; 'cheaper on P/E but grows faster'; throat-clearing 'it's worth noting' / 'needless to say'; 'At today's price, X is priced for...'; 'The valuation arithmetic is [adjective]'; '[Company] generates $X in trailing revenue'; 'Our outlook analysis... leans...'; 'Companies in a similar fundamental position have historically...'; 'flawless execution'/'execute flawlessly'.
gaps.boothcheck_source: prose-gate.mjs (BANNED_TEMPLATE_PHRASES regexes); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §1.4/§1.5/§7.7 (outlook/companies-in-similar-position bans); NEWSLETTER §4 (formulaic openings); TONE-OVERHAUL (ban list from 5-report audit)
gaps.why: design.md §5 has a cliché/metaphor banned-list and §7 bans the 'X is cheaper but Y grows faster' template, but most of these hardened literal templates (throat-clearing 'it's worth noting' IS in §5; the rest are not) are absent. These are finance-flavored so many are NOT for design.md, but the PRINCIPLE 'a recurring literal phrasing becomes a banned template' generalizes and the throat-clearing set ('it's worth noting','needless to say') is already covered. UNDER-SPECIFIED for the general literal-template-ban mechanism.

gaps.theme: Variation — rotate from a shared deck deterministically
gaps.tactic: Assign each item a forced lens/angle from one canonical deck via a seeded function so adjacent items never share a door
gaps.boothcheck_detail: For a set (snippets, a run of posts/reports), draw from ONE canonical angle deck and assign each item a forced lens deterministically (assignAngles(items, DECK, {seed})) so the assignment is reviewable and re-runs are reproducible and the seed drifts which angles lead over time. One shared deck, not a per-item paraphrase. Use a DIFFERENT angle per item in a set. boothcheck even injects two random opening-angle directives per report (12 bull x 10 bear = 120 combos) as a cross-report backstop.
gaps.boothcheck_source: comparison-snippet-voice.md (assignAngles, COMPARISON_LENSES); NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §1 Step 3 (assignAngles, NEWSLETTER_ANGLES, seed=issueDate); TONE-OVERHAUL (randomized opening-angle injection, 120 combos)
gaps.why: design.md §7 covers variation discipline (rotate entry/shape, 3+ = banned template) but does NOT mention the shared-canonical-deck mechanism or deterministic seeded assignment. This is the operational HOW behind variation; partly tooling but the 'one shared deck, rotate per item' principle is a writing rule.

gaps.theme: Variation — recurring theme may recur if entered from a new door and notes how the prior take aged
gaps.tactic: Revisit a theme only from a new angle, referencing how the last read aged
gaps.boothcheck_detail: A recurring theme (e.g. AI-buildout durability) can recur for months as long as each visit enters from a new door and refers to how the prior take aged. Across a series, rotate which angles lead.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §1 Step 3 (Levine freshness); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §7; design.md §7
gaps.why: design.md §7 already: 'A recurring theme is fine to revisit if each visit enters from a new angle and notes how the prior take aged.' Covered.

gaps.theme: Variation — vary the macro structure / section skeleton across a series
gaps.tactic: Rotate the whole skeleton, not just openings: list / single deep-dive / theme / follow-up
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Vary the issue/document structure across the run, not only the opening: default may be a ranked list, but rotate to a single deep-dive, a theme grouping, or a follow-up on how a prior piece aged. Consistency lives in voice and promise, not in an identical skeleton. Paragraph count varies by depth (default 3; simple 2; layered 4-5), not a fixed number.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §1 Step 4 (rotate skeleton); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §5 (paragraph count variable); TONE-OVERHAUL (paragraph-count flexibility); design.md §7
gaps.why: design.md §7 already lists 'vary theme, structure, and angle... opening move, thesis placement, evidence cited, structural-humor frequency, paragraph count, closing move, outlook phrasing' and 'vary paragraph count by the depth the piece needs.' Covered.

gaps.theme: Variation — avoid the metric/evidence trifecta
gaps.tactic: Don't cite the same evidence in the same order across pieces; lead with what's distinctive
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Don't cite the same three metrics in the same order across pieces (the observed ROE → asset-turnover → Piotroski trifecta). Find the 1-2 metrics most DISTINCTIVE about THIS case and vary the evidence cited. Lead with the distinctive (the unusual mechanic, the bet, the moat insight), not the topline.
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §1.3/§5 (moat-metric trifecta); TONE-OVERHAUL (lead with the distinctive); RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §7 (rotate evidence cited)
gaps.why: design.md §7 'vary... evidence cited' across the run covers this. The 'lead with the distinctive not the topline' overlaps §9's arrival-sentence rule. Covered.

gaps.theme: Section discipline — descriptive vs argumentative modes
gaps.tactic: Two modes: argumentative (frame thesis then evidence) vs descriptive (data, no persuasive framing)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Argumentative sections (bull/bear) frame the thesis then give evidence and may carry persuasive framing and a dry edge proportional to subject messiness; descriptive sections (data, valuation, catalysts) state the data and let the reader draw the conclusion, with NO topic sentences that announce the conclusion, no persuasive framing, no editorializing adjectives ('concerning,' 'warranted').
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §10; REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.2; V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (TWO MODES); design.md §9
gaps.why: design.md §9 'Two section modes. Argumentative... frame the thesis, then give evidence. Descriptive... give the data and let the reader draw the conclusion — no persuasive framing.' Covered.

gaps.theme: Section discipline — gate/omit sections when data is absent (no filler)
gaps.tactic: Omit a section that doesn't apply; never pad with self-justifying 'framework does not apply' filler
gaps.boothcheck_detail: A section that doesn't apply must be OMITTED, not filled with filler (e.g. all reports emitting 'this framework does not apply' then defending why — pure padding). Gate conditional sections on real data ('CATALYST_DATA: AVAILABLE | NONE'); without real data the model fabricates 'the next earnings report will be watched closely' filler. Don't invent a section the data doesn't license. 'Better to have nothing than misleadingly confident filler.' Each unit must earn its space (a tight 4 beats a soft 6).
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §1.6/§6 (conditional gating, VALUE_TRAP_CHECK filler); EDITING-PROMPT.md (section gates, don't invent absent sections); NEWSLETTER §1 Step 3 (substance gate); TONE-OVERHAUL (conditional/gated sections)
gaps.why: design.md has no 'omit empty sections / don't pad / each section earns its space' rule. This is a long-form structural-honesty rule that generalizes (don't ship a hollow section just because a template has the slot). Belongs in §9 or §11.

gaps.theme: Citation presentation — quote verbatim or don't attribute
gaps.tactic: If you reword it, it's no longer a quote and can't carry the citation
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Quote source prose verbatim when you want to assert what it says; do not paraphrase and still attach an attribution. Keep the citation glued to the quote (the source identifier is the audit trail). Distinguish using a FACT (verify a number against the source) from using a QUOTE (the source's own words). Never typeset a reconstructed/normalized table as 'verbatim.'
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-FILING-EVIDENCE.md §2/§4 (verbatim-or-don't-attribute; citation glued; prose-faithful vs table-unfaithful); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-RESEARCH (pull-quotes verbatim from existing text); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (verbatim filing quotes)
gaps.why: design.md has no quote-handling rule at all. For any factual/data-bearing user-facing text that quotes a source (a real use-case for the standard), 'verbatim-or-don't-attribute' and 'keep the citation with the quote' are core honesty rules. Belongs in §12.

gaps.theme: Citation presentation — cite primary/first-source with a full, checkable reference
gaps.tactic: Cite primary sources the reader can open; link out; drop aggregators
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Rest credibility on checkable third-party sources, not self-assertion ('third-party sources are more credible than anything you can say yourself'; 'not being afraid to link out is a sign of confidence'). Cite primary/near-primary with a full reference; every number traces to a linked primary filing the reader can open. Drop aggregator citations (reputational risk, no analytical reward); cite the strongest first source. Co-locate the citation with the claim it supports, not in a distant footer.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (cite primary, full reference, link out; co-locate); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.3/§1.9 (verbatim primary quotes, NN/g maxim); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (EDGAR-only, drop aggregators); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (every number to a linked EDGAR filing); UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE (primary-source data)
gaps.why: design.md §12 says numbers must be sourced but never states the presentation rules: cite primary not aggregators, link out, co-locate citation with claim, full reference. These are portable trust-in-prose rules absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Trust in prose — clean, error-free presentation is a first-order credibility lever
gaps.tactic: Correctness and polish carry the trust the content depends on; sloppiness erodes it
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Sloppiness, typos, stale facts, and generic filler erode credibility; clean, professional, error-free presentation is a first-order trust signal (design quality is the most-cited credibility factor at 46.1% of comments — a SALIENCE measure, not 46% of trust). Correctness and polish are not cosmetic. Includes: strip trailing punctuation artifacts, fix pluralization ('1 segments'), don't leave a stale source named, align disclaimer wording to one canonical form.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §1.10 (Fogg 2003, design quality most-cited); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.8 (one off-note = the halo); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (pluralization fix); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (strip trailing periods); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (canonical disclaimer)
gaps.why: design.md treats correctness under accuracy (§12) but never states the trust principle that polish/error-freeness itself carries credibility (one off-note triggers a negative halo). A strong, empirically-backed, portable rule absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Tone — deadpan / uniform emotional register
gaps.tactic: Same emotional register for a catastrophe as for a rounding error; let the subject carry the signal
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Apply the same flat, deadpan emotional register to a fraud or bankruptcy as to a rounding error; the reader picks up the signal from the subject matter, not from authorial emphasis. No editorializing flourish in a closing (no triumphal crescendo); end on a factual, restrained note. Tone may be calibrated by subject quality (clean subject = informed-not-snarky; messy subject = a dry edge allowed) but never swings to alarmism or bravado.
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §1 (deadpan uniform register); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (no triumphal conclusion; consistent register); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.3 (calibrated tone by subject); comparison-snippet-voice (faintly world-weary)
gaps.why: design.md §2 sets 'calm, factual, dry' but does not state the deadpan-uniform-register rule (same register for grave and trivial) nor the no-triumphal-closing rule nor the subject-calibrated dry-edge allowance. Sharpens §2.

gaps.theme: Tone — emphasis must track epistemic confidence
gaps.tactic: Emphasis is a claim about importance; never make the weakest/most-derived claim the most salient
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Emphasis (typographic OR rhetorical) is an editorial claim about importance, so emphasizing the wrong clause misleads. Emphasis/fluency must track confidence, be applied to the strongest-evidence claims, and never make the weakest or most-derived claim the most salient. Differentiate the treatment of an audited fact vs a model-derived estimate. Mis-cueing actively harms (Schneider 2018 STRONG; Reber & Schwarz fluency inflates perceived truth).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §0.3/§1.1/§1.8 (emphasis tracks confidence, mis-cueing harm); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §0.4/§1.8; V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §2 (emphasis tracks the real claim, name the right axis)
gaps.why: This straddles writing and visual, but the PROSE half — don't make your weakest claim the most prominent sentence; reserve emphasis for your strongest-evidence claims — is a writing rule absent from design.md. The typographic half is visual. Flag the prose half for design.md.

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — fluency-truth guardrail / don't manufacture unearned conviction
gaps.tactic: Polish that makes a speculative claim read as crisply as an audited number manufactures unearned conviction
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Ease of processing inflates perceived truth, content-blind (Reber & Schwarz 1999; illusory-truth literature). A presentational/prose rework that makes a SPECULATIVE sentence read as crisply as an AUDITED number manufactures unearned conviction — a real hazard on money/health (YMYL) topics. Never optimize for 'feels credible'/'felt certainty' as a goal; don't let a data-poor result read as confident (mute it, append 'limited data'). Don't over-promise scent or collapse a graded signal into a misleading binary.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §0.3/§1.8 (fluency-truth, double-edge); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §0.4 (never optimize for felt-credibility); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §3.5 (manufactured-conviction trap)
gaps.why: design.md has no fluency-truth guardrail. The principle 'crisp prose can make a weak claim feel true — don't let polish outrun evidence, especially on consequential topics' is a strong, generalizable numeric/honesty rule absent from the baseline. Distinct from G51 (which is about relative emphasis).

gaps.theme: Tone — define the success currency; measure reading/behavior, not belief/activity
gaps.tactic: Don't optimize for shares/virality, felt-credibility, or activity; optimize for honest engaged reading
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Define what success means honestly and refuse the wrong currencies: do NOT make 'feels credible/confident' a KPI (fluency-truth trap), never optimize for shares/virality (~zero correlation with reading), and never optimize for trade/activity (the thing regulators target). Suppress raw small social-proof numbers ('join 80 investors' backfires); prefer specific/expertise-forward proof; a perfect 5.0 reads fake (credibility peaks ~4.0-4.7).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §3 (measure reading not belief/activity; Chartbeat); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §0.4/§1.10 (banned KPI; negative social proof; star-rating peak)
gaps.why: This is mostly a metrics/objective-design rule (what to optimize, social-proof display) — belongs in flow/measurement, NOT design.md prose rules. Flagged as domain=flow so the verifier knows it was considered and routed elsewhere.

gaps.theme: Scope — what NOT to import (we / you / sentence-case / verdict)
gaps.tactic: Report-specific bans (first-person plural, second person, forced sentence case, buy/sell verdict, methodology-leak list) do not apply to brand/marketing copy
gaps.boothcheck_detail: The source canon bans 'we'/'you', forces sentence/lowercase, bans buy/sell verdicts and over/undervalued, and bans methodology mechanism words — but these are report-specific. A brand may say 'we' and 'you'; honor the project's own case; the verdict/methodology-leak bans are finance-product-specific (only the general 'don't leak plumbing' spirit transfers).
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 5 (no 2nd/1st person); comparison-snippet-voice (verdict ban); BRAND.md (no exclamation, no 'we believe'); prose-gate.mjs (smuggling/verdict vocab); design.md 'What NOT to import'
gaps.why: design.md's 'What NOT to import' section already carves out exactly these (no-we, no-you, sentence-case, methodology-leak/buy-sell-verdict). Covered — confirms the baseline correctly rejected the report-only rules.

gaps.theme: Plumbing leak — no internal jargon/mechanism in ANY user surface (incl. tooltips, errors, labels)
gaps.tactic: Banned internal-vocabulary list extends to tooltips, badges, error messages, exclusion-reason copy, not just prose
gaps.boothcheck_detail: The no-plumbing-leak rule covers every user-facing string, not just body prose: tooltips, badge titles, table copy, error messages, exclusion-reason labels must all avoid internal-mechanism vocabulary (iteration counts, model weights/scores, seeds, backtest accuracy, IQR trim, stage-weighted blend, HDI ranges, cellSpec/k-factor/MdAPE/tercile-style jargon). Replace with plain-English reasons. Methodology gets a dedicated home (a concise methodology note), never the decision sections. Frame AI as tooling/capability, never as 'an AI wrote this, trust it less'; never name backtesting/benchmarking publicly ('internally validated'/'calibration on historical data' instead).
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 2 (banned plumbing phrases, methodology dedicated home); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (banned-vocab extends to tooltips/errors/exclusion copy); BRAND.md §7 / BRAND-CAMPAIGN / LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (never name backtesting; AI as tooling); comparison-snippet-voice (no model counts/calibration/state-map); design.md §12
gaps.why: design.md §12 has 'don't leak the plumbing' for reader-facing PROSE and §11 references methodology notes. UNDER-SPECIFIED: the rule that it covers ALL user-facing strings (tooltips/labels/errors), the 'methodology has a dedicated concise home' rule, and the AI-as-tooling-not-confession framing. The specific finance banned-list is correctly NOT for design.md (per 'What NOT to import'), but the scope-extension and AI-framing generalize.

gaps.theme: Word choice — no second person / first person / 'we believe' / editorial adjectives
gaps.tactic: In the report register: no 'you might wonder', no 'we believe', no editorial adjectives doing the number's job
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Banned in the report voice: second person ('you might wonder'), first-person plural ('we believe'), and editorial adjectives doing the number's job ('concerning,' 'impressive,' 'warranted'). Let the number carry the judgment; no editorializing adjective without a figure behind it.
gaps.boothcheck_source: EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 5; NEWSLETTER §2 Pass D; comparison-snippet-voice; REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT (no 'concerning'/'warranted')
gaps.why: The no-we/no-you halves are explicitly carved out by 'What NOT to import.' The editorial-adjective-without-a-number half is covered by §5 ('no intensifier adverb without a number behind it'). Covered.

gaps.theme: Cliché — no intensifier/editorial adjective without a number
gaps.tactic: Drop drastically/massively/remarkably/incredibly/sharply unless a figure makes it literal
gaps.boothcheck_detail: No intensifier adverb or editorial adjective unsupported by a number: drop 'drastically/massively/remarkably/incredibly/sharply/compressed hard/jumped massively/deteriorated sharply' unless a figure makes it literal — rewrite grounded in the actual number.
gaps.boothcheck_source: design.md §5; EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 5; NEWSLETTER §2 Pass D; RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §5
gaps.why: design.md §5 already: 'No intensifier adverb without a number behind it: drop drastically/massively/remarkably/incredibly unless a figure makes it literal.' Covered.

gaps.theme: Cliché — banned dead-metaphor list (fresh metaphors welcome)
gaps.tactic: Ban worn metaphors; coin fresh, concrete, situation-specific ones
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Banned worn clichés/metaphors: headwinds, tailwinds, moving the needle, silver bullet, double-edged sword, uphill battle, taking its toll, rising tide, game-changing, supercharge, unleash, revolutionary, flexing muscles, pulling ahead of the pack, breathing down its neck, execution risk, flawless execution, trough of disillusionment, naked swimmers, flywheel, reversion to the mean. A FRESH concrete metaphor specific to this situation is welcome; the technique is to COIN new images, not reuse old ones.
gaps.boothcheck_source: design.md §5; RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH (Orwell #1, Buffett fresh metaphors); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.7; BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (Report B cliché load); TONE-OVERHAUL
gaps.why: design.md §5 carries the banned list and 'a fresh, concrete metaphor is welcome.' A few extra entries (trough of disillusionment, naked swimmers, flywheel, reversion to the mean) could be appended but the rule is covered.

gaps.theme: Cliché — scare-quote / buzzword inflation as a tell
gaps.tactic: Heavy scare-quoting of invented/borrowed jargon is a low-trust tell; state things plainly
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Leaning on quotation marks around invented or borrowed jargon to lend gravitas ('casual generation', 'isolated islands', 'AI-first ARR', 'naked swimmers') is a marker of the lower-trust register. Strong prose states things plainly without quotation-mark inflation. Untranslated jargon gets translated or deleted, never used raw.
gaps.boothcheck_source: BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (scare-quote inflation tell); NEWSLETTER §4 / comparison-snippet (jargon-laden = failure, translate or delete); design.md §9
gaps.why: design.md §9 bans untranslated jargon ('translate or delete'). The specific 'scare-quoting jargon to borrow gravitas is a tell' rule is not stated. Minor sharpening of §9.

gaps.theme: Hype — no FOMO/urgency/fake scarcity/exclamation/superlative
gaps.tactic: No exclamation marks, no superlatives unless literal, no urgency/countdowns/fake scarcity
gaps.boothcheck_detail: No exclamation marks; no superlatives ('best','dominant','#1') unless a literal on-the-page fact; no FOMO, urgency, countdowns, fake scarcity ('you need to see this','the market is about to'); no fake urgency/hype generally. Confidence reads as a calm, plain statement.
gaps.boothcheck_source: design.md §6; comparison-snippet-voice (hype/superlative ban); NEWSLETTER §4 (fake urgency banned); BRAND.md §5; V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS §0.5
gaps.why: design.md §6 covers all of this. Confirmed.

gaps.theme: Tone — no condescension; explain only the non-obvious
gaps.tactic: Don't talk down; reserve explanation/tooltips for genuinely non-obvious mechanics
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Readers are intelligent. Don't explain the obvious; provide explanation only where the WHAT genuinely needs it. The fix for talking-down is to trust the reader more, not to add hedging/apology. Inline definitions are a glossary job, not a narrative job — name terms cleanly and unpack inline ONLY when the unpack is load-bearing for the next sentence.
gaps.boothcheck_source: UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE (no condescension; explain only non-obvious); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §7.4 (inline-unpack only when load-bearing); TONE-OVERHAUL (inline-definition asymmetry)
gaps.why: Partly overlaps G06 (audience model). The distinct sub-rule here — 'unpack a term inline only when load-bearing for the next sentence; otherwise name it cleanly and let a glossary carry definition weight' — is absent and worth one line. Bundled-but-distinct from G06.

gaps.theme: Per-claim evidence tiering
gaps.tactic: Label every claim's strength-of-evidence (STRONG/MODERATE/CRAFT/DEBUNKED) and quarantine folklore
gaps.boothcheck_detail: In an evidence-bearing writeup, attach an explicit strength-of-evidence label to every claim (STRONG = replicated/meta-analysis/large-N; MODERATE = one/few studies or industry data w/ caveats; CRAFT = designer/practitioner consensus, thin evidence, usable default not 'proven'; DEBUNKED/FOLKLORE = fails replication/misattributed, never cite as support). Labels reflect evidence strength, not the source's rhetorical confidence. Quarantine folklore in a dedicated list so it never sneaks into a recommendation as support. Drop a bad citation even when the underlying move is fine.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (4-tier scheme, quarantine); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (identical scheme); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-RESEARCH (strength-of-evidence required); REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (research-grade vs communication-grade)
gaps.why: design.md has no per-claim evidence-tiering discipline. For research write-ups (an explicit design.md use-case), labeling claim strength and quarantining folklore is a core honesty practice absent from the baseline. Belongs in §12 or a new long-form sub-rule. (Note: this very deliverable USES the scheme.)

gaps.theme: Numeric honesty — reject untraceable/single-source statistics; carry shaky numbers as direction
gaps.tactic: Reject a stat you can't trace to a primary source; carry a shaky magnitude as direction, not a constant
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Where a popular statistic can't be traced to a primary source, REJECT it rather than repeat it (named rejects: '+20% whitespace', '+40% read-time engagement', '+300%/+400% badge lift', '94% of first impressions are design', '17ms appeal', '270%/5 reviews'). When a number traces to a small/old/single study or a measurement artifact, say so and carry the DIRECTION not the constant (Morkes & Nielsen +47%/+124% = 'directional, not a transferable constant'; 57% above-fold = 'artifact not law'). State which findings DON'T transfer to your context. Don't justify a sound move with a debunked statistic — use the real mechanism and drop the bad citation. None of correlational/field data is causal — say so.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (reject untraceable; direction-not-constant; scope/causation caveats; drop bad citation); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (folklore blacklist; ranges-not-precision); REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (flag unsourced thresholds; research-grade vs communication-grade)
gaps.why: design.md §12 covers not-citing-recalled-numbers but NOT: reject-untraceable-statistics, carry-shaky-magnitudes-as-direction, scope/causation caveats, and don't-justify-a-good-move-with-a-debunked-stat. These are distinct, high-value research-honesty rules absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Editing/research process — validate, don't assert; adversarial caveat per claim
gaps.tactic: Pair recommendations with a measurement plan; end every claim with an adversarial caveat; pre-register the metric
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Validate, don't assert: pair claims/recommendations with a measurement plan, pre-register the primary metric and a minimum detectable effect, handle nulls honestly (a null is informative; keep a cheap change even on a null). End every evidence entry with an explicit adversarial caveat that bounds the claim (sample size, era, transfer limits, backfire conditions, survivorship); argue against your own recommendation before the reader can. Define metrics in plain honest terms; state selection effects in any writeup. Run a multi-lens adversarial review; instruct the skeptic to reject false positives. Blind-review on the merits (de-identify, read in isolation, score on named axes).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (validate-don't-assert; pre-register; adversarial caveat per entry; honest null); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (pre-register; adversarial review; selection effect); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT §7 (multi-lens review, skeptic rejects false positives); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (blind de-identified review, 5 axes); REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (null is publishable; no mid-program reframing)
gaps.why: design.md §11 covers prose edit passes but NOT the research-process discipline: validate-don't-assert, pre-register the metric, adversarial-caveat-per-claim, honest null handling, no-mid-program-reframing-to-rescue-a-story, blind review on the merits. For research write-ups (a design.md use-case) this is a major absent block. Largely writing-adjacent research method; flag the writeup-facing parts for design.md.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — no mid-program reframing to rescue a story; one metric named up front
gaps.tactic: If results disappoint, write a null; don't switch target/metric post-hoc; pick one primary metric and commit
gaps.boothcheck_detail: If results are disappointing, write up a null result; do NOT reframe to a different target or metric to rescue the story (a post-hoc goalpost move). Pick a single primary metric up front and commit; a second metric is allowed only as a labeled cross-check, never an alternate headline. Ban whole classes of flattering metrics in advance. Prefer the honest (robust/median) metric when outliers dominate. Catch and rewrite your own overstated prior headline into the truthful framing.
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (no mid-program reframing; one metric; ban flattering metric classes; honest metric; rewrite overstated headline); REPORT-QUALITY-OVERHAUL (cite the matching base-rate bucket not the flattering floor)
gaps.why: design.md has no anti-goalpost-move rule. For data-bearing/research writing this is a sharp, generalizable honesty rule (don't change the claim after seeing the result) absent from the baseline. Belongs in §12.

gaps.theme: Anti-smuggling — surface a claim reversal, don't silently retcon
gaps.tactic: When new evidence contradicts a prior claim, flag the reversal explicitly rather than quietly editing it
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When a new finding reverses something you already wrote/claimed, surface the reversal explicitly (its own note) rather than silently overwriting the earlier text. Cross-section consistency: the same figure/claim must agree everywhere; if two sections disagree, surface the discrepancy as a finding, don't average it away. Carry citation corrections into the record and instruct they not be reverted.
gaps.boothcheck_source: REPORT-FV-ACCURACY-RESEARCH (surface reversal, cross-horizon divergence is a finding); EDITING-PROMPT.md Rule 4 (cross-section consistency); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (carry citation corrections, don't revert)
gaps.why: design.md §12 requires cross-document consistency but not the active 'surface a reversal / disagreement is itself a finding / carry corrections forward' discipline. Distinct from passive consistency. Worth a clause in §12.

gaps.theme: Editing process — single-source shared copy so prose and summary can't drift
gaps.tactic: Define each piece of explanatory copy once; reuse verbatim so short and long forms can't disagree
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Define each blurb/explanatory string in ONE place and reuse it verbatim across surfaces (chip + prose, summary + body) so the short form and long form state the same thing and cannot drift. Same-metric drift (10.7% vs 10.9% across sections) breaks trust; reconcile to one canonical value.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §3.2 (single-source SEVERITY_PLAIN, reuse verbatim); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (numeric drift 10.7 vs 10.9; KEY METRICS block use-verbatim)
gaps.why: This is an implementation/tooling discipline (single-source the string in code) more than a prose-authoring rule. The prose-facing half (the same claim must read identically in summary and body) overlaps §12 cross-document consistency. Routed mostly to tooling; flagged so the verifier sees it was considered.

gaps.theme: Citation presentation — disambiguate self vs source section numbers; preserve acronym casing
gaps.tactic: Disambiguate your own section numbers from a cited source's; preserve proper-noun/acronym casing
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When prose mixes its own section numbers with a source's, disambiguate explicitly ('all §x cites refer to Google's Guidelines, not this document'). Preserve acronym/proper-noun casing even inside stylistically-lowercased containers (SEC must not render 'sec').
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.1 (disambiguate section numbers); UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE (acronym casing preserved)
gaps.why: Niche but real citation-clarity rules absent from design.md. The acronym-casing one is partly visual/CSS; the section-number disambiguation is a prose-clarity rule. Low priority but exhaustive-listing requires it.

gaps.theme: Word choice — rename a metric/label whose name no longer matches what it does
gaps.tactic: A label that no longer describes the thing is a misnomer; rename at the display layer
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Honesty-in-naming: a label that no longer matches what it names ('Bank Fair Value' used for non-bank cells) is a misnomer and should be renamed at the display layer to describe what it actually does ('Tangible Book Anchor'). No inverted/backwards labels (a label must mean what the number means). Prefer descriptive over jargon names in user-facing labels ('Forward Multiple Valuation' over 'Discounted Future Market Cap').
gaps.boothcheck_source: V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (rename misnamed metric); V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL (no inverted labels; qualify every metric); REPORT-POLISH-TODO (jargon-vs-retail naming standard)
gaps.why: design.md has no label-honesty rule. The principle 'a label must accurately name what it is; rename a misnomer; prefer descriptive over jargon labels; no inverted labels' generalizes to any in-product strings (a design.md scope item). Worth a line.

gaps.theme: Word choice — qualify every metric / period basis
gaps.tactic: Never an unqualified 'today' or bare metric name; label which quantity and which period
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Every metric must be qualified so the reader always knows exactly which quantity and period: 'operating margin' not bare 'margin'; 'now' vs 'mid-cycle' vs 'trailing' not an unqualified 'today'; label TTM/FY/QoQ/YoY and GAAP-vs-non-GAAP and est-vs-actual on every figure; full-year figures must not be mislabeled quarterly.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-HEADER-REDESIGN-PROPOSAL §1/§4.4 (qualify every metric/period); BLIND-REPORT-AB-TEST (period labels TTM/FY/QoQ/YoY; GAAP vs non-GAAP; est tags); EDITING-PROMPT.md (full-year vs quarterly); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (periodized estimates carry their period); design.md §12
gaps.why: design.md §12 'Label the period and unit of every forward/estimated figure; never mix units silently' + 'Label every comparator's denominator (trailing vs forward vs current).' The bare-metric-name and GAAP/est tags are minor extensions; the period/unit core is covered.

gaps.theme: Trust in prose — don't fix density/length by editing the prose; length is not the enemy
gaps.tactic: Walls of text can be correct prose; the fix for digestibility is presentational, not a rewrite
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Density/length is not automatically a prose defect: high-quality long prose presents as a wall of text but the fix is presentational (navigation, layering, scannable anchors interleaved with prose), NOT shortening/punch-up/dumbing-down. Optimize 'scannable + invites deep reading,' not 'shorter.' More cited sources is a feature, not bloat. (Preserve prose verbatim when only the wrapper changes.)
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-RESEARCH (length not the enemy; preserve verbatim, change the wrapper); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS (edit-vs-regenerate: change only the wrapper); design.md §11
gaps.why: This is primarily a reading-LAYOUT principle (fix engagement via presentation, not by cutting prose). The writing-facing half (don't shorten good prose for its own sake) lightly overlaps §11 edit-don't-regenerate. Routed to reading-layout; the 'don't shorten for its own sake' nuance is the only writing-relevant slice and is minor.

gaps.theme: Opening structure — subject line / headline craft (front-load, concrete, tight)
gaps.tactic: Subject lines/headlines: concrete, conversational, front-load first ~40 chars, under ~60 total
gaps.boothcheck_detail: A subject line/headline must be concrete (the reader learns something), conversational, and tight: front-load the first ~40 characters and keep it under ~60 total. (Concrete numbers preserved.)
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §2 Pass E (subject line: front-load ~40, under ~60)
gaps.why: design.md covers openings/taglines generally but has no subject-line/headline craft rule with the front-load-40/under-60 heuristic. Since email/share-copy/launch-post headlines are explicitly in design.md scope, this concrete rule is a real gap. Belongs in §9 or §7.

gaps.theme: Scannability
gaps.tactic: Skim reader gets the point in ~5 seconds; key number/ticker visible at a glance; short paragraphs
gaps.boothcheck_detail: The text must be scannable: the one key fact/number visible at a glance, short paragraphs, a skim reader gets each unit in ~5 seconds. Descriptive (not clever) headings act as 'pickup points' producing the layer-cake gaze; labels must match section contents (deceiving scent is worse than weak scent). The F-pattern is a FAILURE symptom of unformatted text — break it into layer-cake.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER §2 Pass E (~5s per unit); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §1.2/§1.6 (descriptive headings, layer-cake, accurate scent)
gaps.why: Mostly reading-LAYOUT (headings, scannability, gaze patterns) — belongs in reading-layout/visual, not design.md prose rules. design.md §11 pass E mentions 'scannability' as an edit pass but the heading-craft/layer-cake/accurate-scent material is presentation. Routed to reading-layout.

gaps.theme: Visual posture — deadpan voice forbids consumer-app chrome
gaps.tactic: No gamification, drop caps, decorative pull-quotes, confetti, countdowns; hairline/no-card report typography
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Any engagement device that reads as consumer-app chrome spends credibility: ban gamification (confetti/badges/points/streaks), drop caps/ornamental initials, magazine-style decorative pull-quotes duplicating prose, countdowns/urgency. Use report-style hairline (no-card) typography. Be prototypical in structure, differentiated in substance (novelty goes in the analysis, not the chrome).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §4B (banned decorative devices); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md §1.8 (prototypical structure); LIABILITY-HONESTY-AUDIT (no-card hairline typography); BRAND.md / UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE
gaps.why: Pure VISUAL/presentation rules — belong in VISUAL-DESIGN.md, not design.md (which is prose/voice). Flagged so the verifier knows these were considered and routed to the visual core. The 'novelty in substance not chrome' maxim has a faint writing echo (G44) already captured.

gaps.theme: Reading comfort — constrain measure, paragraph rhythm
gaps.tactic: ~60-72 chars per line, real whitespace between paragraphs; easier-looking text raises willingness to continue
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Cap the prose column to ~60-72 chars (~34-38em) and give paragraph breaks real air; over-long lines are least preferred. Easier-LOOKING text lowers predicted effort and raises willingness to continue (presentational, framed as honesty-in-comfort, not a comprehension claim).
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md §2.2 (measure ~60-72 cpl; Dyson 2004)
gaps.why: Reading-LAYOUT/typography — belongs in VISUAL-DESIGN.md / reading-layout, not design.md prose rules. Routed accordingly.

gaps.theme: Document structure — orientation up front (how-to-read map + closing synthesis)
gaps.tactic: Open a long doc with a how-to-read map and per-section job; close with a synthesis restating the through-line
gaps.boothcheck_detail: A long research doc opens with a 'how to read this' map that states each section's job before the reader enters it (and the evidence-tier legend), and closes with a short synthesis that restates the shape of the answer and names the cheapest/highest-leverage moves and the single genuine fork — synthesis is given, not left for the reader to assemble. Pin recommendations to concrete verified artifacts (file+line), no hand-waving. Prioritize by an explicit scoring rubric.
gaps.boothcheck_source: V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-FINDINGS.md (how-to-read map; closing synthesis; ground-truth table; scoring rubric); V5-TRUST-CONVERSION-FINDINGS.md (§0 framing-first; per-section job)
gaps.why: design.md §9 covers openings (arrival sentence, BLUF) but NOT the long-research-doc conventions: a how-to-read map, an evidence legend, and a closing synthesis that does the assembly for the reader. For research write-ups (a design.md use-case) these are real structural rules absent from the baseline.

gaps.theme: Rhetoric — progressive thesis resolution across a document
gaps.tactic: State the thesis at increasing resolution: high-level summary → evidence-backed → final reframe
gaps.boothcheck_detail: State the thesis at different levels of resolution across the document — high-level in the summary/takeaways, refined with specific evidence in the body, reframed one last time in the closing (repetition-as-persuasion). NOTE boothcheck considered but did NOT ship this as an explicit instruction (risked over-constraining the variation it was trying to preserve).
gaps.boothcheck_source: RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH-APR15.md §5/§11 (progressive refinement, considered-not-shipped); V5-REPORT-ENGAGEMENT-RESEARCH (BLUF + layering)
gaps.why: design.md §3 has the clarity-kick (a closing reframe) and §9 BLUF, but not the explicit 'thesis at escalating resolution across the whole doc' arc. boothcheck itself flagged this REJECTED (over-constrains variation), so it's a borderline add — note it but it may be deliberately omitted.

gaps.theme: Few-shot anchors — teach shape, not substance
gaps.tactic: Worked examples in a standard should transfer structure/voice, never be lifted as content
gaps.boothcheck_detail: When a standard/prompt includes worked examples (anchors), they teach SHAPE/voice only — guard against verbatim copying ('use the SHAPE not the substance'). Anchors should deliberately demonstrate DIFFERENT orderings so the writer learns variation rather than one template. (A meta-rule about how the standard itself uses examples.)
gaps.boothcheck_source: TONE-OVERHAUL (4 anchors COST/WMT/NEE/OKLO, copy shape not substance); REPORT-NARRATIVE-AUDIT §4 (anchors demonstrate different orderings); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (anchors teach shape without contaminating)
gaps.why: design.md uses examples (the em-dash rewrites) but never states the meta-rule that examples teach shape not substance and should show varied orderings. A useful authoring principle for the standard, low priority.

gaps.theme: Cadence / output budget
gaps.tactic: Sustainable cadence; give generation enough token budget so sections don't truncate; watch prompt-length budget
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Operational guardrails: pick a sustainable cadence (weekly anchor); give generation enough token budget so the final section doesn't truncate mid-sentence (4096→8192); watch the prompt-length budget (more rules is not free — trim if the model starts missing rules).
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER-WRITING-PROCESS.md §0.5 (cadence); V1-REPORT-INTEGRATION (max_tokens 8192 no-truncation); TONE-OVERHAUL (prompt-length budget watch)
gaps.why: Operational/tooling (cadence, token budgets, prompt size) — not a prose/voice rule. Belongs in tooling, not design.md. Flagged for completeness.

gaps.theme: Scannability — translate-or-delete jargon (failure mode)
gaps.tactic: Jargon used without translation is a failure: translate it or delete it
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Untranslated jargon ('secular tailwind','TAM expansion','operating leverage') is a named failure mode: translate it into plain meaning or delete it, never use it raw.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER §4 (jargon-laden failure); comparison-snippet / RHETORIC-AND-VOICE-RESEARCH §9; design.md §9
gaps.why: design.md §9 'Untranslated jargon ("secular tailwind," "TAM expansion") gets translated or deleted, never used raw.' Covered.
